articles.title: Assam's growth: Sarma meets FM, inaugurates Syama Prasad flyover
articles.description: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma met Union FM Nirmala Sitharaman to discuss the state's development. He also inaugurated the ₹376 crore Dr. Syama Prasad Mookerjee Flyover in Guwahati and took oath for his 6th term as MLA.

articles.title: Maha govt warns against fuel stockpiling amid price hikes, says CM
articles.description: Maharashtra Minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule announced strict measures against fuel stockpiling, citing CM Fadnavis's orders. He also warned that MCOCA would be invoked against those involved in cow slaughter ahead of Eid al-Adha.

articles.title: Aquarius, Weekly Horoscope, May 24 to May 30, 2026: Week brings mood fluctuations and financial movement
articles.description: Early week brings mood fluctuations and financial movement, though results may not meet expectations. Midweek requires caution in daily activities and communication due to restlessness. By Thursday and Friday, career prospects brighten with unexpected earnings and good news related to children, leading to recognition over the weekend.

articles.title: Retail fuel breaches ₹115-mark in Andhra Pradesh as successive hikes pinch consumers
articles.description: Retail fuel prices in Andhra Pradesh surpass ₹115 per litre, raising concerns for consumers and affecting logistics and dealers.

articles.description: Telangana has the potential for a thriving tourism economy but needs cohesive branding and improved infrastructure for growth.

articles.title: New ROB to be constructed at Valacode
articles.description: General drawing and alignment for constructing a new Railway Over-Bridge (ROB) on the National Highway at Valacode, Punalur, have been submitted to the Railways after successfully completing the joint inspection, N. K. Premachandran, MP has said.

articles.title: Petrol, prices and pressure: How the Iran war is hitting American households
articles.description: Escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have triggered a surge in US fuel prices, pushing inflation to a three-year high and eroding wage gains. While the US economy has shown resilience with steady consumer spending and job growth, persistent inflation threatens household budgets and consumer sentiment, with higher bond yields signaling deeper economic worries.

articles.title: Centre releases draft rules for VB-GRAM G Act, 2025; invites public consultation ahead of July 1 rollout
articles.description: The Centre has released draft rules for the VB-GRAM G Act, 2025. Public consultation is now open for feedback. This is a crucial step before the Act's scheduled rollout on July 1. The government aims for a smooth implementation. More details on the Act's provisions will follow the consultation period.

articles.title: 6 home cooling essentials to beat the summer heat​
articles.description: Air coolers help circulate cool air while consuming less electricity than air conditioners. They work especially well in dry climates and can make bedrooms and living spaces feel significantly cooler. Air cooler

articles.title: Vedanta, MCX, among 10 stocks that saw highest increase in retail shareholding in Q4. Do you own any?
articles.description: Amid heightened market volatility driven by the Iran conflict, elevated crude oil prices and persistent foreign investor selling, retail investors continued to selectively increase exposure to several domestic stocks during the March 2026 quarter. Data from Primeinfobase shows investors gravitated towards sectors such as metals, energy, industrials and capital markets, with a number of stocks also delivering strong gains during the period. Here are top 10 names. Retail Rise

articles.title: A Shift Towards Cleaner Cooking Makes Cold‐Pressed Oils Better In The Indian Kitchen
articles.description: Cold‐pressed oils are extracted using mechanical pressure at relatively low temperatures, and this helps the oil stay closer to its natural form, making it healthier for you.

articles.title: India-Estonia relations on strong growth path, says Ambassador Sinha
articles.description: India and Estonia are witnessing a 'strong trajectory of growth' in bilateral relations across trade, digital cooperation, and cyber security, says Ambassador Ashish Sinha, noting a 150% increase in Indian exports to the Baltic nation.

articles.title: Green Transition Holds 'Greatest Long-Term Potential' For India Ties, Says Norway FM | Exclusive
articles.description: In an exclusive conversation with CNN-News18, Norway's Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide said green energy, shipping, and innovation hold the greatest promise for India-Norway ties

articles.title: The High Cost of Hubris
articles.description: Kerala's ambitious SilverLine rail project has been officially cancelled, marking a significant political shift. The government has halted land acquisition and withdrawn cases against protesters. This decision follows overwhelming evidence of the project's financial, environmental, and technical non-viability, despite initial government assurances. Experts and citizens had repeatedly raised concerns about its feasibility and impact.

articles.title: Cannes 2026: Aishwarya Rai Bachchan stuns in soft pink couture while daughter Aaradhya dazzles in bold ruby-red gown- See pics
articles.description: The iconic mom and daughter duo delivered a memorable style contrast at Cannes 2026 with soft romantic hues paired against bold dramatic styling, making their appearance one of the standout highlights of the evening.

articles.title: RailTel Corp bags Rs 31-cr IT infrastructure upgrade order from NSIL
articles.description: RailTel Corporation of India said that it has received a work order from NewSpace India (NSIL) for supply, installation, commissioning, and operation & maintenance of upgradation of IT infrastructure.

articles.title: Colgate-Palmolive India Q4 PAT edges lower to Rs 353 crore
articles.description: Colgate Palmolive (India) reported 0.47% marginal decline in standalone net profit to Rs 353.32 crore in Q4 FY26 as against Rs 355 crore in Q4 FY25.

articles.title: Hindalco posts record Q4 revenue and EBITDA; PAT drops 51% YoY on Novelis fire-related exceptional losses
articles.description: Hindalco Industries reported record consolidated revenue and EBITDA for Q4 FY26, supported by strong performance across its India aluminium and copper businesses.

articles.title: Fuel Prices Jump For Third Time In 10 Days; Is A Bigger Hike Around The Corner?
articles.description: Petrol and diesel prices rose again for the third time in 10 days as soaring crude oil prices and OMC losses pushed rates higher.

articles.title: LRS outflows up over 10% in March led by travel demand
articles.description: Indians sent $2.59 billion abroad in March 2026 via the Liberalised Remittance Scheme. This marked a 10.9% increase from the previous month. Travel spending saw a seasonal dip. However, investments in equity and debt surged significantly. Deposits abroad also showed a strong recovery. Remittances for education moderated after a peak in January.

articles.title: Supply-side stress, weather add uncertainty to macros
articles.description: India's economy faces near-term challenges. Supply issues and weather create uncertainty. Geopolitical events in West Asia could impact domestic prices. Despite these concerns, domestic demand remains strong. The Reserve Bank of India is monitoring inflation closely. Robust exports and policy measures offer support against external risks.

articles.title: India Rupee shows strong rebound on firm RBI support
articles.description: On Friday, the Indian rupee made a notable leap, finishing the day above the 96 per dollar threshold. The surge was largely attributed to robust actions from the Reserve Bank of India, following a dip towards 97 mid-week. Traders noted a wave of dollar offerings from state-run banks, part of a broader strategy to stabilize the rupee's performance.

Petrol, diesel prices hiked for third time this month on rising crude oil costs
articles.description: Fuel prices in India have seen another increase, with petrol and diesel rates rising by approximately Rs 0.87 and Rs 0.91 per litre respectively in Delhi. This marks the third hike this month, driven by a surge in international crude oil prices due to Middle East conflict and the expiry of a US sanctions waiver on Russian oil.

articles.title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked for third time in eight days on rising crude oil costs
articles.description: Fuel prices in India have seen another increase, with petrol and diesel rates rising by approximately Rs 0.87 and Rs 0.91 per litre respectively in Delhi. This marks the third hike this month, driven by a surge in international crude oil prices due to Middle East conflict and the expiry of a US sanctions waiver on Russian oil.

articles.title: Planning SIPs for a car or house in 10 years? Experts recommend diversified equity funds for long-term goals
articles.description: Financial experts advise first-time SIP investors to focus on diversified equity mutual funds for long-term goals, avoid panic during volatility, limit exposure to sectoral funds, and rely on professional fund managers for balanced asset allocation and disciplined wealth creation over time.

articles.title: China News live updates: Eight dead, 38 trapped in China coal mine accident
articles.description: Eight people were confirmed dead and 38 remain trapped underground after a coal ‌mine ⁠accident ⁠in northern China's Shanxi province, state news agency Xinhua reported on Saturday, citing local ⁠emergency management ‌authority.

articles.title: Graphs, Data and Perspectives: Why rising govt bond yields are bad news
articles.description: Globally, government bond yields are rising, and rising sharply. This has an impact on consumers and businesses, and how they borrow money.

articles.title: Rs 69,000 for answer sheets: CBSE portal glitch leaves students, parents baffled; re-evaluation timeline extended
articles.description: CBSE Class 12 students face post-result anxiety due to glitches in revaluation portals, including fluctuating fees and inaccessible services. The board has extended deadlines and cited unprecedented traffic and unauthorized interference for disruptions. Students report issues with website crashes, payment failures, and unclear scanned answer sheets, leading to widespread frustration and calls for intervention.

articles.title: Gold on track for second weekly loss as rising oil prices drive up rate hike bets
articles.description: Gold prices are falling, heading for a second weekly loss. Higher oil prices are fueling inflation worries and boosting chances of a U.S. interest rate hike. Treasury yields are near one-year highs, reducing demand for gold. Other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium are also experiencing losses. Consumer sentiment has hit a record low.

articles.title: UAE tightens wage payment rules for private sector firms from June 1
articles.description: Starting June 1, 2026, UAE private sector employers must pay salaries by the first of each month via the Wage Protection System or authorized channels. The compliance threshold for wage transfers will increase to 85%, and a new dispute resolution mechanism for delayed payments will be introduced.

articles.title: 8,054 revenue officials to be appointed soon
articles.description: In a significant move to address land disputes, the Bihar government plans to hire 8,054 revenue officials, aimed at resolving an enormous backlog of around 40,000 cases. Minister Dilip Jaiswal has unveiled plans to revitalize the Bihar Land Tribunal, expanding its team to seven members.

articles.title: Night flight operations at Darbhanga airport soon: Jha
articles.description: Exciting news for travelers! Darbhanga Airport is gearing up for night flight operations, thanks to a productive meeting between JD(U) leader Sanjay Kumar Jha and Defence Minister Rajnath Singh. The Union minister has guaranteed robust support, including extra Air Force personnel and technical help, to make night landings a reality.

articles.title: Tejashwi targets NDA over inflation, weakening rupee and unemployment
articles.description: In a recent statement, RJD leader Tejashwi Yadav launched a scathing attack on the NDA government, pointing to soaring inflation rates and rampant unemployment as indicators of its failing economic strategies. He expressed deep concern over the escalating prices of basic necessities that are straining the finances of families—especially the less affluent and middle-class citizens in Bihar.
